Calculate median gene CV within each raw-data group

Source: R/QC.R
calc_group_median_gene_cv_from_exp_file.Rd

Read the same raw expression table accepted by rawdata2se(), parse sample conditions using the same sample-name rule, calculate each gene's CV across replicates within each condition, and return the median CV per condition. Missing values are ignored.

Usage

calc_group_median_gene_cv_from_exp_file(
  exp_file,
  obs_col = "Genes",
  raw_prefix = "auto",
  group_by = "condition"
)

Arguments

exp_file

Path to the raw expression table file.

obs_col

Name of the column in exp_file used to define feature identifiers. Only the substring before the first semicolon (;) will be used as the feature name.

raw_prefix

Character string used to identify raw expression columns. If "auto" (default), raw intensity columns are detected using the same filename-style suffix rules as rawdata2se().

group_by

Column in the parsed sample metadata used for grouping. Default is "condition".

Value

A data.frame with one row per group, including the median gene CV and the 95th percentile CV across replicated samples in that group.

On this page
